groups.description: Groups is the list of the User's Groups.

match.description: Match contains a single CEL expression

all.description: All acts as a logical AND operator on its list of CEL expressions

any.description: Any acts as a logical OR operator on its list of CEL expressions

not.description: Not acts as a logical NOT operator of a single CEL expression

none.description: None acts as a logical NOR operator on its list of CEL expressions (i.e. none of the expressions matches)

opa.description: OPA contains an OPA (Open Policy Agent) Rego script

of.description: Expressions is the list of CEL expressions
Expressions is the list of CEL expressions

inline.description: Inline is the OPA Rego script directly provided as a string

status.description: Status is the current status of the Device.

policies.description: Policies is the list of standalone Policies

inlinePolicies.description: InlinePolicies is the list of inline Policies

userRef.description: UserRef is a reference to the owner User

status.description: Status is the current status of the Group.

policies.description: Policies is the list of standalone Policies

inlinePolicies.description: InlinePolicies is the list of inline Policies

name.description: Name is the name of the inline Policy

status.description: Status is the current status of the Namespace.

attrs.description: Attrs is a map user-defined attributes, mostly used in authorization rules. It is strongly recommended to stick to camelCase in order to be conformant with Octelium's API naming conventions.

policies.description: Policies is the list of standalone Policies

inlinePolicies.description: InlinePolicies is the list of inline Policies

rules.description: Rules is the list of Policy rules

enforcementRules.description: EnforcementRules is the list of enforcement rules that are typically used as a precondition for using the Policy rules. You can simply extract your shared conditions from your rules and write them instead inside enforcementRules for better performance and readability

isDisabled.description: IsDisabled disables (i.e. deactivates) the Policy. You can use this to temporarily disable enforcing the Policy altogether instead of having to delete it or de-attach it from the different Resources attaching it.

condition.description: Condition is the enforcement rule's Condition

name.description: Name is an optional name for the rule. It is currently useful in Logs to understand which rule triggered the Policy.

condition.description: Condition is the rule's Condition that must match in order for the rule, and subsequently the Policy as whole, to match

priority.description: Priority is an integer that sets the rule priority level. The lower the number, the higher the priority. Currently the range of numbers is between -4 (i.e. the highest priority) and 4 (i.e. the lowest priority).

http.description: HTTP is the HTTP specific details.

headers.description: Headers is the map of HTTP request headers.

path.description: Path is the HTTP path without query params.

host.description: Host is the HTTP request `Host` or 'Authority` header value.

protocol.description: Protocol is the HTTP protocol (i.e. `HTTP/1.0`, `HTTP/1.1` or `HTTP/2`)

size.description: Size is the HTTP request size in bytes. If unknown, it must be -1.

bodyMap.description: BodyMap is the serialized request body. Currently used with JSON.

uri.description: URI is the request path including the query

queryParams.description: QueryParams is the map of HTTP request query params. Params only assumes single-valued query params. If you want to check multi-valued params then you need to instead parse the URI field.

service.description: Service means that this is a Service scope

api.description: API means that this is an API scope

all.description: All means allow access to all APIs

filter.description: Filter filters access to certain APIs or certain methods of certain APIs

packages.description: Packages is the list of gRPC packages

services.description: Services is the list of gRPC services

methods.description: Methods is the list of gRPC methods

all.description: All means allow access to all Services

filter.description: Filter filters access to certain Services or certain Namespaces

names.description: Names is the list of allowed Service names

namespaces.description: Namespaces is the list of allowed Namespace names

status.description: Status is the current status of the Service.

port.description: Port is the port number used by the listener. If not set, Octelium will try to assume the number from the backend URL. (e.g. if the backend's URL scheme is `http` then the port number is 80).

config.description: Config sets the listener's application-layer specific options.

dynamicConfig.description: DynamicConfig sets the dynamic configuration options. DynamicConfig can simply dynamically override the global/static Service configuration via programmable CEL rules that take identity and context into account.

isTLS.description: IsTLS enables TLS for the listener using its Namespace certificate (i.e. You have to enable TLS at the Namespace level in order to enable this field).

isPublic.description: IsPublic enables the client-less public "BeyondCorp" mode access for the Service. Only works for HTTP-based Service modes for now.

isAnonymous.description: IsAnonymous turns a publicly exposed HTTP-based Service into an anonymously exposed Service that can be anonymously accessed publicly. In other words, this turns off authentication and authorization for the Service. This requires the isPublic field to be enabled too. Refer to the docs for more.

deployment.description: Deployment sets The Service's underlying deployment configurations.

isDisabled.description: IsDisabled disables the Service. Simply the Service stops accepting requests altogether as if it is not running.

region.description: Region explicitly sets the Region name in which the Service is deployed.By default, a Service is deployed in the "default" Region.

policies.description: Policies is the list of standalone Policies

inlinePolicies.description: InlinePolicies is the list of inline Policies

name.description: Name is a unique name the describes the Config. Only needed for Configs described within the DynamicConfig. The default Config is assumed to have the name "default".

clientCertificate.description: ClientCertificate sets mTLS-related configuration (i.e. the client x509 certificate chain and its corresponding private key) to authenticate to an upstream that requires mTLS. This is unused for certain modes such as SSH since it does not use mTLS.

upstream.description: Upstream is the Service detailed upstream information. Only one of the URL or Upstream must be set.

parent.description: Parent is the name of the parent configuration

header.description: Header sets request and response header manipulation options

path.description: Path sets the request path options

cors.description: CORS sets the Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)-specific options. Read more at https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers.

isUpstreamHTTP2.description: IsUpstreamHTTP2 sets HTTP connections to the upstream to HTTP/2. Not enabled by default.

listenHTTP2.description: ListenHTTP2 sets the Service to listen to HTTP 2.0 connections. This is only enabled by default for Services using TLS. This field has to be enabled in the in the "default" or global Configuration (as opposed to named dynamic Configs) in order to actually work.

enableRequestBuffering.description: EnableRequestBuffering buffers the entire request body before sending it to the upstream. This can be used to check the request body content for access control and dynamic configuration. This means that it has to be enabled in the "default" or global Configuration (as opposed to named dynamic Configs) in order to actually work.

plugins.description: Plugins is the list of plugins

visibility.description: Visibility sets the visibility/access logging specific options

custom.description: Custom sets authentication inside custom header

oauth2ClientCredentials.description: OAuth2ClientCredentials sets OAuth2 client credentials authentication flow details

sigv4.description: Sigv4 sets the AWS signature v4 authentication details

username.description: Username is the username value of HTTP basic authentication

password.description: Password is the password value of HTTP basic authentication

header.description: Header is the name of the HTTP header (e.g. "X-Custom-Auth")

value.description: Value is the value of the header

clientID.description: ClientID sets the OAuth2 client ID

clientSecret.description: ClientSecret sets the OAuth2 client secret

tokenURL.description: TokenURL sets the token URL endpoint

scopes.description: Scopes is the list of OAuth2 scopes

maxRequestSize.description: MaxRequestSize sets the max body request byte size.

allowOriginStringMatch.description: AllowOriginStringMatch is the list of  patterns that match allowed origins.

allowMethods.description: AllowMethods specifies the content for the access-control-allow-methods header.

allowHeaders.description: AllowHeaders specifies the content for the access-control-allow-headers header.

exposeHeaders.description: ExposeHeaders specifies the content for the access-control-expose-headers header.

maxAge.description: MaxAge specifies the content for the access-control-max-age header.

allowCredentials.description: AllowCredentials specifies whether the resource allows credentials.

addRequestHeaders.description: AddRequestHeaders is the list of request headers to be added.

removeRequestHeaders.description: RemoveRequestHeaders is the list of request headers to be removed.

addResponseHeaders.description: AddResponseHeaders is the list of response headers to be added.

removeResponseHeaders.description: RemoveResponseHeaders is the list of response headers to be removed.

append.description: Append appends the header value instead of setting/overwriting/replacing it which is the default behavior.

addPrefix.description: AddPrefix adds a prefix to the request path.

removePrefix.description: RemovePrefix removes a prefix from the request path. You can use both RemovePrefix and AddPrefix at the same time to replace a prefix by another one.

addPrefix.description: AddPrefix adds a prefix to the request path.

removePrefix.description: RemovePrefix removes a prefix from the request path. You can use both RemovePrefix and AddPrefix at the same time to replace a prefix by another one.

enableRequestBodyMap.description: Capture the request body map, currently JSON

enableResponseBodyMap.description: Capture the response body map, currently JSON

kubeconfig.description: Kubeconfig sets the cluster configuration from a kubeconfig. Only one of ClusterInfo or Kubeconfig must be set.

user.description: User is the postgres user. If set, it overrides the user values requested by the downstreams. If not set, the user values requested by the downstreams is forwarded to the upstream as is.

auth.description: Auth sets the upstream authentication information

database.description: Database is the name of the database. If set, it overrides the database values requested by the downstreams. If not set, the database values requested by the downstreams is forwarded to the upstream as is.

user.description: User is the SSH user. If set, this value overrides the SSH user requested by the downstreams. If not set, then downstream's value is forwarded to the upstream.

upstreamHostKey.description: UpstreamHostKey sets the upstream's host public key

eSSHMode.description: eSSHMode enables the "embedded SSH" mode. This means that the upstream is served by an Octelium client rather than a typical SSH server. Refer to the docs for more.

password.description: Password sets the password used for authentication. Only one of Password or PrivateKey is used.

privateKey.description: PrivateKey sets the private key used for authentication. Only one of Password or PrivateKey is used.

insecureIgnoreHostKey.description: InsecureIgnoreHostKey ignores verifying the upstream server public key during the handshake. This is extremely NOT recommended for use in production

key.description: Key specifies the upstream public key

user.description: User is the User name if the upstream should be served by an active Connection of a User. If set, that means that the Service is served by any connected client owned by that User and willing to serve the Service.

url.description: URL is the canonical URL of the upstream. Examples are`http://example.com`, `postgres://pg.default.svc`, `tcp://my-custom-app:9090`,  and `https://api.sandbox.paypal.com`.

loadbalance.description: Loadbalance is the list of upstreams in case you have more than one upstream. You either use the main upstream or the loadbalance list.

container.description: Container sets the Upstream to a managed container deployed on the same Kubernetes cluster that hosts the OCtelium Cluster

port.description: Port is the port exposed by the managed container

image.description: Image is the image URL of the container

command.description: Command overrides the container image's command

args.description: Args is the list of command arguments that override the image arguments

env.description: Env is the list of environment variables

replicas.description: Replicas is the number of replicas of this container

credentials.description: Credentials sets the credentials if the image is hosted on a private container registry

resourceLimit.description: ResourceLimit sets the container resource limits

securityContext.description: SecurityContext sets k8s security context related configs

usernamePassword.description: UsernamePassword uses a username and password to authenticate to the container registry

username.description: Username is the username used to authenticate to the container registry

password.description: Password is the password used to authenticate to the container registry

server.description: Server is the container registry server (e.g. "ghcr.io" or "quay.io")

fromSecret.description: FromSecret sets the name of the Secret whose value contains the Password

name.description: Name is  the environment variable key

value.description: Value is the environment variable value

fromSecret.description: FromSecret gets the value of the environment variable from an Octelium Secret

kubernetesSecretRef.description: KubernetesSecretRef uses a Kubernetes secret as the environment variable value

name.description: Name is the Kubernetes secret name

key.description: Key is the Kubernetes secret data key

ext.description: Ext represents the map of extended/custom resource limits such as GPU requests

millicores.description: Millicores is the integer that sets the limit milli-cores

megabytes.description: Megabytes is the integer that set the limit in megabytes

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: ReadOnlyRootFilesystem sets the root filesystem to read-only

runAsUser.description: RunAsUser sets the UID of the container user.

endpoints.description: Endpoints is the list of endpoints

url.description: URL is the canonical URL of the upstream. Examples are`http://example.com`, `postgres://pg.default.svc`, `tcp://my-custom-app:9090`,  and `https://api.sandbox.paypal.com`.

user.description: User is the User name if the upstream should be served by an active Connection of a User. If set, that means that the Service is served by any connected client owned by that User and willing to serve the Service.

replicas.description: Replicas sets the number of replicas of a deployed Service.

configs.description: Configs is the list of named configurations that will override the global/default Service configuration if a rule matches.

rules.description: Rules is the list of rules that are checked on a per-request basis, if a rule matches its named configuration is used as the Service configuration for that specific request.

configName.description: configName is the name of the namedConfig that is used if the rule matches.

eval.description: Eval is the CEL expression that evaluates the Service config

opa.description: OPA is the Rego OPA script that evaluates the Service config

addresses.description: Addresses is the list of private addresses used by the Service for client-based connections.

namespaceRef.description: NamespaceRef is the reference of the owner Namespace

status.description: Status is the current status of the Session.

expiresAt.description: ExpiresAt is the timestamp at which the Session expires.

policies.description: Policies is the list of standalone Policies

inlinePolicies.description: InlinePolicies is the list of inline Policies

userRef.description: UserRef is the reference to the User of the Session

deviceRef.description: DeviceRef is the reference to the Device of the Session if available

isBrowser.description: IsBrowser means that the Session is used by a browser.

authentication.description: Authentication includes the information of the authentication operation used currently by the Session

lastAuthentications.description: LastAuthentications is the list of last Authentications

initialAuthentication.description: InitialAuthentication is the initial Authentication of the Session

isConnected.description: IsConnected shows whether the Session has a connected client. Only useful for CLIENT Sessions.

connection.description: Connection shows the connection information of the currently connected Session

scopes.description: Scopes is the list of scopes used by the Session

upstreams.description: Upstreams is the list of upstreams actually served by this connected Session

addresses.description: Addresses is the list private addresses used by the connected Session's client

serviceOptions.description: ServiceOptions shows the configuration of the served Services

x25519PublicKey.description: X25519PublicKey is the raw x25519 public key of the Connection. Currently used by WireGuard-based connections.

ed25519PublicKey.description: Ed25519PublicKey is the raw ed25519 public key of the Connection. Currently used for eSSH.

publishedServices.description: PublishedServices is the list of published Services to the connected client host

ignoreDNS.description: IgnoreDNS means that the connected client does not use the Cluster DNS

eSSHEnable.description: ESSHEnable means that serving eSSH Services is enabled

eSSHPort.description: ESSHPort is the port number of the eSSH Server. Only meaningful if ESSHEnable is set to true

serveAll.description: ServeAll means that the Session is willing to serve any Service that can be served by the Session's User

portStart.description: PortStart is the starting listen port used by the client to serve its Services

host.description: Host is the host of the actual upstream

port.description: Port is the listen port of the actual upstream

status.description: Status is the current status of the User.

groups.description: Groups is the list of Group names that this User belongs to. You must not add the `root` Group as it's only used by the `root` User or the `all` Group as it's already added by the Cluster automatically.

email.description: Email sets the default e-mail for the User. It is used for User authentication if the IdentityProvider returns the email value in the authentication information (e.g. OIDC identity tokens, SAML 2.0 assertions or GitHub OAuth2). The e-mail is used as a fallback authentication method if there are no User identity that matches in the

info.description: Info is the User information. Only used for HUMAN Users currently.

isDisabled.description: isDisabled de-activates/disables the User. Once disabled, the User cannot interact with the Cluster or access its Services until this field is set to false again.

identities.description: Identities is the list of explicit User identities

identityProvider.description: Provider is the provider's name according to the Cluster configuration.

identifier.description: Identifier is the value that identifies the User account according to the provider. (e.g. the username in the case of Github and Gitlab, the email for Google, OpenID Connect, SAML 2.0, etc...)

policies.description: Policies is the list of standalone Policies

inlinePolicies.description: InlinePolicies is the list of inline Policies

clientDuration.description: ClientDuration sets the Session duration used by clients after which the Session is deemed expired and automatically deleted by the Cluster.

clientlessDuration.description: ClientlessDuration sets the Session duration for the client-less/BeyondCorp mode after which the Session is deemed expired and automatically deleted by the Cluster.

accessTokenDuration.description: AccessTokenDuration sets the access token duration

refreshTokenDuration.description: RefreshTokenDuration sets the refresh token duration

maxPerUser.description: MaxPerUser sets the max number of of Sessions per User

v4.description: V4 is the IPv4 network address represented as CIDR notation IP address and prefix length (e.g. `192.0.2.0/24`).

v6.description: V6 is the IPv6 network address represented as CIDR notation IP address and prefix length (e.g. `2001:db8::/32`).

milliseconds.description: Milliseconds is the duration set in milliseconds

seconds.description: Seconds is the duration set in seconds

minutes.description: Minutes is the duration set in minutes

hours.description: Hours is the duration set in hours

days.description: Days is the duration set in days

weeks.description: Weeks is the duration set in weeks

months.description: Months is the duration set in months

uid.description: UID is the unique UUID value for the object assigned by the Cluster. Read-only.

name.description: Name is the name of the object. It must be unique within a certain API/Version/Kind tuple.

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is the timestamp of creation of the object. Read-only.

updatedAt.description: UpdatedAt is the timestamp of when the object was last updated. Read-only.

displayName.description: DisplayName is the name that can be shown publicly for the object. Doesn't need to be unique.

description.description: Description is used to describe the object in a short sentence. Maximum characters are 1000.

labels.description: Labels is a map of string keys and values to organize objects.

tags.description: Tags is an optional array of tags that classify the Resource.

annotations.description: Labels is a map of string keys and values that can be used to store metadata about the object.

picURL.description: PicURL is the Picture URL of the resource.

resourceVersion.description: ResourceVersion is a unique ID that represent the current version of the resource object. Used by the Cluster to detect updates.

lastResourceVersion.description: LastResourceVersion is the ResourceVersion of the last update of the Resource.

actorRef.description: ActorRef is the object reference to the Actor resource, typically a Session, who is responsible for the current resource change. If ActorRef is not set, then the current change of the resource was done by the Cluster

actorOperation.description: ActorOperation is the gRPC full method triggered by the actorRef to modify the Resource

isSystem.description: IsSystem means that the resource is created by the Cluster and it cannot be managed by the administrators. Read-only.

specLabels.description: SpecLabels is map used internally by the Cluster for quick searches among lists of objects. Read-only.

isUserHidden.description: IsUserHidden means that the resource object is hidden from ordinary Users and should not be exposed via the User APIs.

isSystemHidden.description: IsSystemHidden means that the resource object is hidden by the Cluster and should not be exposed via the APIs.

systemLabels.description: SystemLabels is a similar map to specLabels. Used only by the Cluster.

apiVersion.description: APIVersion is the API version of the reference resource

kind.description: Kind is the resource kind. A kind is unique per an APIVersion.

uid.description: UID is the resource object UID. A UID is unique across the entire Cluster.

name.description: Name is the resource object name. A name is unique per a certain kind.

resourceVersion.description: ResourceVersion is the resourceVersion of the resource.
